title: Fatal: dealloc clears the in-flight exception in `subtype_dealloc` (`typeobject.c:2719`)
description: `Objects/typeobject.c` `subtype_dealloc` tears down a pure-Python instance's slots and `__dict__` (decref'ing attribute values) without saving/restoring `tstate->current_exception`. The exception-preserving wrapper exists only in the `__del__` path (`slot_tp_finalize`); types with no `__del__` (`tp_finalize == NULL`) skip it. Under OOM such an instance is freed with a `MemoryError` in flight during frame unwinding, the teardown clears it, and the gh-89373 `_Py_Dealloc` debug invariant fatals. ONE root for the whole non-`Context` family.
notes: ONE generic root cause, not per-type. Nine vehicles across four pure-Python stdlib types (_StoreAction/argparse, UnknownHandler+ProxyHandler/urllib.request+xml.sax.saxutils, LogRecord/logging) all emit the identical 'cleared the current exception' fatal; none defines __del__, so all inherit subtype_dealloc and share the defect. subtype_dealloc (typeobject.c:2719) preserves the in-flight exception ONLY via slot_tp_finalize (typeobject.c:11209/11235, _PyErr_Get/SetRaisedException), which runs only when tp_finalize is set (i.e. a __del__ exists). With no __del__ the slot/dict teardown (clear_slots L2840, PyObject_ClearManagedDict L2847, Py_DECREF(dict) L2854) decrefs attribute values under a pending MemoryError and clears tstate->current_exception, tripping the Py_DEBUG-only _Py_Dealloc invariant (object.c:3338). Distinct from OOM-0007 (Context): same symptom/class but a DIFFERENT C site/fix location -- OOM-0007 is the dedicated context_tp_dealloc in Python/context.c; this is the generic subtype_dealloc in Objects/typeobject.c, so Context is excluded and reported separately. Fix: bracket subtype_dealloc's destructive section with _PyErr_GetRaisedException/_PyErr_SetRaisedException; one change covers the whole family. Matrix: debug-only invariant. Fatals deterministically on ft_debug_asan AND jit (jit is also Py_DEBUG=1 and surfaces the identical '_StoreAction cleared the current exception' fatal 10/10, like OOM-0007 -- the earlier-draft claim that jit segfaults at re._compiler._optimize_charset was wrong, verified by re-run). ft_release/upstream compile the invariant out (NDEBUG) and do NOT surface this signature (clean exit or an unrelated downstream OOM segv) -> n/a. Minimization: YES -- stdlib-only sweep over http.server._main([]) with imports warmed; crashes ~start=276 (single fixed start does not reproduce; the sweep is required). DEDUP (2026-06-23): added per-type msg keys for ALL four family types (UnknownHandler/ProxyHandler/LogRecord, alongside the original _StoreAction) so any family vehicle dedups by its type-specific fatal message. Needed because removing the generic _Py_Dealloc key left the backtrace fallback resolving to the now-keyless _Py_Dealloc dispatch frame (chain[0]) -> a non-_StoreAction vehicle (e.g. urllib ProxyHandler on the magalu GCC box) was mislabelled oomNEW. The msg is the real family discriminator (the backtrace frame-teardown site PyStackRef_XCLOSE@726/_PyFrame_ClearLocals@101 is shared with OOM-0005, so it can't disambiguate).
